text: This Al Jazeera report summarises Western officials’ claims that Russia conducted a cyberattack against the KA-SAT satellite network, causing widespread communication outages in Ukraine and several European countries. It cites EU and US statements describing the incident as malicious activity aimed at undermining Ukrainian command and control, while also affecting services such as wind farms and civilian internet users. The article places the attack within the broader context of Russian cyber operations during the war and highlights the political condemnation and calls for accountability issued by European leaders.
